definition: The capacity to accept or tolerate delay, trouble, or suffering without getting angry or upset
example: How long, Catiline, will you abuse our patience? How long will that madness of yours mock us?

definition: The state of being mentally ill, especially severely
example: How long, Catiline, will you abuse our patience? How long will that madness of yours mock us?

definition: The faculty by which the mind stores and remembers information
example: The life of the dead is placed in the memory of the living. Not to know what happened before you were born is to remain a child forever.

definition: Lack of knowledge or information; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: Ignorance is the hinge of Orator ad M. Brutum: without it the rest is decoration.

definition: The crime of betraying one's country; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: A nation can survive its fools, and even the ambitious. But it cannot survive treason from within.

definition: The health, happiness, and fortunes of a person or group
example: The welfare of the people is the supreme law.

definition: The state of being free within society from oppressive restrictions
example: Liberty is the hinge of Philippica II: without it the rest is decoration.

definition: Faithfulness to a person, cause, or belief, demonstrated by continuing loyalty and support
example: Nothing is more noble, nothing more venerable than fidelity.

definition: A cruel and oppressive ruler; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: Tyrant is the hinge of Philippica II: without it the rest is decoration.

definition: Just behavior or treatment; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: Justice is the hinge of In Verrem: without it the rest is decoration.

definition: Dishonest or fraudulent conduct by those in power; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: Corruption is the hinge of In Catilinam I: without it the rest is decoration.

definition: The state of great comfort and extravagant living; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: The enemy is within the gates; it is with our own luxury, our own folly, our own criminality that we have to contend.

definition: The quality of being faithful and dependable; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: Constancy is the hinge of De Officiis: without it the rest is decoration.

definition: Admirably purposeful, determined, and unwavering; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: It is the character of a brave and resolute man not to be ruffled by adversity and not to be elated by prosperity.

definition: A state in which supreme power is held by the people and their elected representatives
example: In a republic, the voice of the people, when it is the voice of reason and justice, must prevail.

definition: Behavior showing high moral standards; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: Virtue is the hinge of De Officiis: without it the rest is decoration.

definition: Cause (someone) to do something through reasoning or argument
example: The appeal was not to wait, but to persuade—Antidosis made delay look like surrender.

definition: A legally recognized subject or national of a state or commonwealth
example: We are not born for ourselves alone, but for our country, our friends, and our fellow citizens.

definition: High respect; great esteem; a word that gains force in public speech.
example: Honor is the hinge of De Officiis: without it the rest is decoration.

definition: A secret plan by a group to do something unlawful or harmful
example: Conspiracy is the hinge of In Catilinam I: without it the rest is decoration.

definition: Fluent, forceful, and persuasive speaking or writing.
example: Cicero treated eloquence as moral architecture—beauty built to carry truth.
